generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring the robot motor input current, preprocessed with a low‑pass filter"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentTransmitted
]

instance MotorAngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring the angular velocity of the robot motor, filtered before transmission"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngularVelocityTransmitted
]

instance JointAxisPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring the position of the first robot axis, filtered before transmission"
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointAxisPositionTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (Data transmitted)
instance MotorCurrentTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorAngularVelocityTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngularVelocitySensor
]

instance JointAxisPositionTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointAxisPositionSensor
]

// C10: Data (post‑processing)
instance CleanMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered motor current data ready for digital twin ingestion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance CleanMotorAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered motor angular velocity data ready for digital twin ingestion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngularVelocityTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance CleanJointAxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered first‑axis position data ready for digital twin ingestion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointAxisPositionTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

// C10: Models
instance WhiteBoxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics‑based white‑box friction model implemented in MathWorks Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data‑driven black‑box friction model realized as a neural‑network regressor"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

// Model output data (optional)
instance WhiteBoxFrictionOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction estimate produced by the white‑box Simulink model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction estimate produced by the black‑box neural‑network model"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MqttBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker that converts filtered sensor data to JSON and publishes on hierarchical topics"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionComputationService,
                     JointFrictionCompensationService,
                     PredictiveMaintenanceService,
                     MachiningParameterOptimizationService,
                     CentralDataMemoryService
]

instance AasExecutorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) Executor that ingests data, runs friction models and hosts the digital shadow on ThingsBoard"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionComputationService,
                     JointFrictionCompensationService,
                     ExternalForceCompensationService,
                     PredictiveMaintenanceService,
                     MachiningParameterOptimizationService,
                     CentralDataMemoryService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionComputationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computes real‑time joint friction using both white‑box and black‑box models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionPredictionInsight
]

instance JointFrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatically compensates joint friction in the robot controller based on computed friction"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomaticFrictionCompensationAction
]

instance ExternalForceCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Compensates external machining forces using digital twin predictions"
    DTDFVocab:provides ExternalForceCompensationAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects anomalies in friction behaviour for early fault detection and maintenance planning"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight,
                     MaintenanceAlertAction
]

instance MachiningParameterOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs simulated experiments to optimise machining parameters for quality and CO₂ reduction"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimisationResultInsight,
                     ParameterAdjustmentAction,
                     CO2ReductionRecommendationInsight,
                     ProductQualityPredictionInsight
]

instance CentralDataMemoryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides a central data repository (digital shadow) that downstream applications can query"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataMemoryInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance FrictionPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time friction estimate for each joint, derived from combined model outputs"
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection result indicating potential friction‑related fault"
]

instance OptimisationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimal set of machining parameters identified by simulation experiments"
]

instance CO2ReductionRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendation for reducing CO₂ emissions based on optimized parameters"
]

instance ProductQualityPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Prediction of product quality outcomes derived from twin simulations"
]

instance DataMemoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Information about stored data availability for downstream applications"
]

// C17: Actions
instance AutomaticFrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic command sent to the robot controller to compensate predicted friction"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ExternalForceCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of controller set‑points to counteract external machining forces"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Notification sent to maintenance personnel when a fault is detected"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to update machining parameters in the production line"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates under real‑time requirements: live sensor data from the robot is streamed continuously via MQTT to a ThingsBoard instance, where it is processed and fed to simulation models with minimal dead time in the control loop. Synchronization is performed as soon as new measurements become available, aiming for low‑latency updates that keep the computational delay shorter than the control system’s allowable dead time, although no explicit frequency or latency values are given."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a pipeline‑based orchestration: an Asset Administration Shell (AAS) encapsulates the robot’s SysML structural model and executable Simulink behavioral models (integrated through FMI). MQTT transports live measurements to a Processing component that runs the friction simulation models; results are stored in ThingsBoard and automatically visualised on a dashboard. Resource management relies on the AAS‑based container and the ThingsBoard platform, while the pipeline autonomously triggers model execution, data aggregation, and visualization, providing system‑wide coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin follows a Model‑Based Systems‑Engineering (MBSE) lifecycle that is explicitly split into four abstraction levels (Document 1): (1) Requirements modeling – captured with SysML requirements diagrams; (2) Functional architecture – described by block diagrams and interface specifications; (3) Logical architecture – instantiated as component‑connector models using ADLs such as ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML internal block diagrams; (4) Product/technical layer – concrete implementation models (e.g., Simulink, C++, Java) that are executed at runtime. Transition between stages is managed by traceability links defined by the “originates‑from” association (Document 2), which allow each data trace to be traced back to the processing component, input traces and the models that generated it, thereby supporting lifecycle governance and change management across the stages."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is maintained by continuous verification of the transformation chain from SysML to Simulink or to machine‑learning pipelines, as highlighted in the literature. Traceability links enable detection of deviations between predicted and observed data, triggering automatic parameter updates or neural‑network retraining when new friction measurements are received. Sources of uncertainty such as sensor noise and model simplifications are acknowledged, and quality assurance is achieved by preserving provenance metadata for each data trace, applying verification methods for model consistency before deployment, and ensuring that real‑time requirements and control‑system dead‑time constraints are respected."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection relies on an MQTT broker implementing the MQTT protocol’s lightweight, message‑based, publish‑subscribe architecture. Topics are organized hierarchically and can be assigned read/write permissions. Data are transmitted as JSON payloads. The AAS Executor component subscribes to the relevant MQTT topics to receive sensor data, while the broker also forwards data to the ThingsBoard platform for storage and visualization. No explicit network topology, bandwidth, latency, redundancy, or security specifications are provided beyond the use of MQTT and hierarchical topic permissions."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is achieved through Industry 4.0 standards: the Digital Twin communicates with physical devices via MQTT, stores and visualises data in ThingsBoard, and incorporates the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for semantic interoperability. The integration pattern connects the twin to external enterprise applications (e.g., production planning, CO₂‑reduction tools) by exposing the centralized data memory and model outputs through the AAS and ThingsBoard APIs, enabling other systems to consume and augment the twin’s data."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system complies with established modeling standards and reference‑model concepts: SysML (systems modeling language), AADL (SAE Architecture Analysis & Design Language), ACME, and MontiArc are used as specification languages for architecture and behavior (Documents 1, 4). Conformance relations defined in reference models (Document 2) ensure that concrete realizations adhere to the abstract specification, providing an interoperability framework across heterogeneous tools and stakeholders. No specific industry certification or ISO‑type standards are mentioned."
]
